top_screaming.text:
HIS LORDSHIP’S MASTER
HIS LORDSHIP’S MYSTERIES
BOOK 2
SAMANTHA SORELLE
31 OCT 2021

5 ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ — EXCELLENT STORY AND ADVENTURE!
5 ♥️♥️♥️♥️♥️ — LOVED THIS BOOK!
5 🤙🤙🤙🤙🤙 — RECOMMEND IT TO EVERYONE!

I LOVED THIS BOOK AS MUCH AS I DID THE PREQUEL AND BOOK ONE.

I LOVED THE MYSTERY, ADVENTURE AND LOVE!
MOST OF ALL THE UNERRING, UNDYING LOVE — NO MATTER WHAT.

I DO SO HOPE THERE ARE MORE OF THESE TO COME IN THE FUTURE.

MS SORELLE DOES SO WELL WRITING ALL THINGS EMOTIONAL — ALL THINGS LOVE.
OF COURSE, HER SKILLS WITH MYSTERY AND ADVENTURE AND WILD RIDES ARE NEARLY UNEQUALLED COMPARED TO ANY OF THE WRITERS I’VE BEEN READING ON KINDLE.
YES . . . UNEQUALLED.

THIS IS THE CONTINUING STORY OF THE BOXER AND THE EARL BEYOND LONDON.
THEY DECIDE TO HEAD TO THE SEAT OF ALFIE’S EARLDOM IN SCOTLAND — TO BALCARRES HOUSE.
JUST FOR THE FUN OF IT — ALONG THE WAY THEY STOP AT THE MANY SMALLER PROPERTIES THAT ALFIE GAVE TO NICK, NOT ONLY TO CHECK THAT ALL IS WELL WITH THE PROPERTIES BUT ALSO, TO HAVE A LOT OF ONE ON ONE ALONE TIME TOGETHER — AS IT TAKES WEEKS TO DO THIS ON THE WAY TO SCOTLAND.

WHEN THEY DO FINALLY ARRIVE IN SCOTLAND AND AT BALCARRES HOUSE THEY ARE VERY SHOCKED — AS INDEED, IT WAS NOT AT ALL WHAT THEY WERE EXPECTING.
AND THINGS JUST GO FURTHER DOWNHILL FROM THERE.

THEY CAN FEEL THERE US SOMETHING VERY “NOT RIGHT” ABOUT THE PLACE.
AND SOON, ONCE AGAIN, THEY’RE INVOLVED IN MYSTERIES, TALES OF OLD, HAUNTED PASSAGEWAYS, “THE WICKED MASTER,” LITTLE RED BOOKS, QUIRKY SERVANTS, ACTUALLY, DECIDEDLY HATEFUL SERVANTS, MYSTERIOUS PAINTINGS AND MURDER — WELL, MAKE THAT MORE THAN ONE MURDER.

YUP, UP TO THEIR CRAVATS IN IT ALL, ONCE AGAIN!
AND WHAT A GREAT ADVENTURE MS SORELLE HAS PENNED FOR ALL OF US TO DELIGHT IN.

JUST ABSOLUTELY SUPERB, AGAIN!

HOWEVER, TO MY DISAPPOINTMENT, AT LEAST ONE THREAD WAS LEFT OPEN.
IT WAS BROUGHT UP ONLY BRIEFLY (and, when it was, I was so anxious to see where it was going to lead) BUT, AFTER IT WAS BROUGHT UP IT WAS NEVER MENTIONED AGAIN — THUS, MS SORELLE LEFT OPEN (at least to me she did) AN UNRESOLVED QUESTION IN THIS STORY — “ . . . vanished money from the Balcarres house funds . . . “ STOLEN BY MR GIBSON — AND, WHETHER HE WAS MURDERED, OR NOT, THAT DID NOT RESOLVE THE DISCOVERY OF THE THEFT AND AFTER IT WAS DISCOVERED IT WAS NEVER AGAIN BROUGHT UP AGAIN, NOR WAS IT RESOLVED.
WHY?
I KNOW THEY ONLY BROUGHT UP THIS THIEVERY AS A POSSIBILITY AS TO WHY MR GIBSON WAS MURDERED, HOWEVER, I THINK THEY SHOULD HAVE PURSUED THIS LINE OF THE PLOT (maybe in the next book?) BECAUSE IT .I.S. MISSING AND HE HAD TO HAVE PUT THAT MONEY SOMEWHERE (and it had to have been used — or going to be used — for some thing. Hopefully, and not just taken for the pure “greed” of it.) AND IT REALLY BELONGED TO THE EARL AND HIS “BALCARRES HOUSE.”
HOW LONG HAD MR GIBSON BEEN STEALING AND HOW MUCH HAD HE ACCUMULATED AND WHERE HAD HE KEPT IT?

OH! AND THE HOUSE TUNNEL THAT LEAD TO THE OUTSIDE?
THE ONE THAT MALCOLM USED TO ESCAPE HIS FATHER, ALL THE TIME?
DID IT STILL EXIST?
AND, WHAT ABOUT ALL THE OTHER TUNNELS MENTIONED IN MALCOLM’S “LITTLE RED BOOK?”
DID THEY STILL EXIST?
I KNOW THE OUTSIDE EXIT TUNNEL COULD PROBABLY BEEN SEALED OFF WITH THE NEW STONE ADDITIONS — MAYBE NOT — AS CHARLETON NEVER MENTIONED FINDING THEM WHEN HE HELPED WITH THE NEW WALL.
BUT STILL, MALCOLM’S DIARY MENTIONED “SEVERAL” TUNNELS BESIDES THE ONE HE USED TO ESCAPE THE MANOR, AND THE CURRENT HOUSEHOLD ONLY KNOWS OF TWO INTERIOR ONES — WELL, MAKE THAT THREE, NOW!

PLUS, I DON’T WANT NICK TO GET RID OF THE LANDS (that he feels he doesn’t know how to manage) THAT ALPHIE SO UNSELFISHLY GAVE TO HIM!!!
PLEASE, COME UP WITH A DIFFERENT SOLUTION THAN THE ONE OFFERED AT THE END OF THIS BOOK!
THEY’VE BEEN IN ALPHIE’S FAMILY FOR GENERATIONS, IT WOULD SEEM UNGRATEFUL AND WOULD BE REALLY DISAPPOINTING.
LET NICK EITHER LEARN TO MANAGE THEM, HIRE AN ESTATE MANAGER, LET ALPHIE MANAGE THEM ALONG WITH HIS OWN — OR — GIVE THEM BACK TO ALPHIE!
BUT, PLEASE, DO NOT LET HIM GET RID OF OR SELL THEM OFF!
PLEASE!?!

OOPS ‼️‼️‼️
I GUESS THAT MAKES THREE THINGS I’D LIKE TO SEE RESOLVED.

HMMM . . . ALL TO BE ANSWERED IN ANOTHER BOOK, OR BOOKS, MAYBE?

BUT, DON’T LET MY WANTS FOR WHAT WASN’T IN THIS BOOK, STOP YOU FROM TREATING YOURSELF TO WHAT IS IN THIS BOOK AND TO ANOTHER GREAT ADVENTURE OF SAMANTHA SORELLE’S.

YOU REALLY DON’T WANT TO MISS THIS BOOK — AT ALL!

WELL WORTH ANYTHING I MAY HAVE WISHED FOR — IT’S NOT MY BOOK — SHE CAN GO ANY DIRECTION SHE WANTS — AND, YES, I WILL GLADLY FOLLOW!!!

YES . . . PLEASE, GIVE US MORE, MS SORELLE!

PLEASE?!?
AND, THANK YOU, AGAIN!

🌷🌷🌷MTULIPK🌷🌷🌷
top_screaming.title: I LOVED THE MYSTERY, ADVENTURE AND LOVE! MOST OF ALL THE UNERRING, UNDYING LOVE — NO MATTER WHAT.

top_five_star_obscene.text:
A stubborn hero with an attitude, a tenacious heroine with moxy, meddling family members, a bit of baggage, smart and sometimes funny dialog and intense chemistry all made The Bastard Billionaire one fantastic story.

Just in case you were wondering (as I was), Elijah Crane, the hero in The Bastard Billionaire isn’t technically a bastard, his parents were married, but I’m guessing that the publisher didn’t want to call this book, The Grouchy A$$hole Billionaire. The thing is he really isn’t all that bad, he’s working to accept how his life has changed since the explosion that took his two friend’s lives, cost him his leg, and got him discharged from the Marines. He’s back in Chicago with his well meaning family that wants him to assume a position at the family company that he’s not sure he wants all while working on a charity to help other disabled veterans. He has scared off ten professional temporary administrative assistants and now Isabella (Sable) Sawyer, the owner of the temp agency, is going in and plans to show Elijah who is boss, but neither of them was prepared for the immediate attraction and respect they have for one another.

I do so love it when a heroine can manage to knock the hero down a peg or two from the get go mainly because he thinks he knows exactly how to make someone uncomfortable enough to get rid of them. I loved both these characters even though Elijah was difficult. It was obvious he had PTSD not only from his recent injury, but he was still harboring guilt from an incident in his young life. Emotionally he was closed off and afraid to let anyone in and this of course caused strife not only with his family but with Sable as their relationship progressed. I liked the Sable pushed Elijah’s boundaries all the time and that she called him out when he was behaving badly, I also like that when she realized she had made a critical error in dealing with Elijah that she apologized.

This was a busy book with a lot of side stories all weaving around Elijah and Sable as they try and figure out their relationship and build the businesses that mean so much to them but I never felt this book lost sight of this couple. Despite this book had a bit more angst than I normally like, given Elijah’s baggage it was not gratuitous and he had enough people in his life to tell him to give over himself, admit his wrong doing, accept his fate and move on. You have to love the tough love in this family!

There were a lot of special moments in this book beyond those spent between Elijah and Sable. Elijah’s brothers, their significant others, his father and a couple of friends all added so much to this story and gave us good insight into understanding this complex man.

With The Bastard Billionaire author Jessica Lemmon takes this series out on a high note, as sad as I am to see it go I’m hoping we might see some of the characters in this book spin-off into another series.

Review copy provided for a voluntary review.
top_five_star_obscene.title: he Bastard Billionaire author Jessica Lemmon takes this series out on a high note,

top_five_star_obscene.text: Excellent work, very well written with a lot of good background material.  I won't get into all the details, you can find that elsewhere, other than to mention that the original story influenced Herman Melville's book, Moby Dick. As my old English Professor used to say: "Moby Dick is Not just a fish story."  Oh Boy, was he right.  Philbrick also has a book out titled, Why Everyone Should Read Moby Dick, obviously I'm in agreement.  BTW, Jed McKenna's interpretation of Melville's Moby Dick Odyssey is a real eye opener if anyone is interested.  Also wanted to mention that Ron Howard's movie version of In The Heart Of The Sea is a good rendition of the book.  I found it to be a fairly accurate portrayal of the story with only minor differences for dramatic effect, but that's another review.  Sadly, it probably isn't mainstream enough to be a long running hit.
top_five_star_obscene.title: More that just a fish story.

top_five_star_obscene.text:
Silver Bastard (Silver Valley #1)
By: Joanna Wylde
5/5 stars

Puck Redhouse was a prospect for the Silver Bastards MC during Reaper’s Stand (Reapers MC #4) and he shadowed London for some time and helped figure out the plan when she was going to kill Picnic in order to save her niece’s life. He also went to prison in California for 14 months with Painter, a member of the Reapers MC.

The Prologue starts out in the timeframe of “5 years before” on the day that Puck & Painter are released from prison and going to a party that night before they head back home to Idaho. That night, Puck sees Becca and he is immediately taken by her, wants to meet and has to have her. But there are things going on behind the scenes that both the Reapers & Bastards feel are out of place. When Puck learns the truth about Becca & her situation, he steals her away.

After that, the timeframe jumps to present day. I was a little bit disappointed, because I felt like we got cheated out of Becca’s healing, getting to know Earl & Regina even more. Also, we there weren’t any scenes of Puck putting a “generic” claim on her & having her under club protection, and how he felt compelled to watch over her. I’m not saying I needed the full 5 years, but I would have liked some of the in between times. But with the 5 year jump, this allows Becca to grow out of her teenage years, make decisions for herself and her future and stand on her own.

I loved the scenes that contained Painter & how we got to learn about snippets of his life in the last 5 years since getting out of prison!! They worked well with this book because of the close friendship that Puck & Painter built while in prison. These scenes also contained some “teaser” scenes that you know will show up in Painter & Mel’s book, Reaper’s Fall (publishing Nov. 2015) and I’m even more excited for it!

One thing that I didn’t like was that we didn’t really learn anything new about Puck’s background. We did learn that he tagged along behind his dad & the Silver Bastards, spent time growing up in bars, didn’t really remember his mother & was an only child. At no point did Puck & Becca have a talk about their backgrounds. Of course Puck knew about Becca’s background from the time that he met her, and not anything before, but the readers are given a glimpse of that. In a way, it worked because Puck is an alpha-hole and doesn’t really put up with/have patience for much and is extremely direct, almost to the point of being insensitive and uncaring. But Puck truly cares about Becca and wants to take care of her, now and in the future. Later on, he does say that if something disturbs her mentally that will cause problems for them, that he needs to know. But they never have that conversation at the beginning of their reconnecting, so there isn’t any way for her to know that.

I absolutely loved the history of the towns, the silver mines and the “world” of Callup & the Silver Valley. It was entirely new to me & I could just see all the rich history!
top_five_star_obscene.title: Puck the Alpha-hole & the Silver Valley

top_five_star_obscene.text:
I’ve been so desperate to read Bastard Prince ever since finishing off the first book, Royal Bastard.

Book two continues where book one left us. That major cliffhanger at the end of book one was surprising and Bastard Prince has even more surprises in store for you.

Lucas and Bryna are even more amazing in this book. We get to see more of Lucas’ struggles here and to say it hurts is an understatement. It feels like someone is clutching your heart in a tight grip and just won’t let it go. The aftermath of his childhood still haunts him and has him making decisions that aren’t exactly the best for him. Bryna is as badazz and awesome as ever. I love how strong she is but her heartbreak was horrible to witness. Wanting someone so bad but thinking they don’t want you as much as you want them is heartbreaking, poor girl.

Lucas and Bryna’ journey was like a rollercoaster ride. It hits you with an insane amount of feels. One minute you’re happy them next your heart is hurting and that, to me makes an excellent book. The twists and turns were quite refreshing. I didn’t see most of what happened coming and I was legit in shock, just wow.

Nana Malone does an incredible job of getting you hooked to her books. Once I started I just couldn’t stop until I had finished and I’m sure glad I didn’t because this beauty was well worth staying up to 3:23am to finish.

Bastard Prince is full of secrets, twists and turns and sex, lots and lots of freaking hot sex. I love me some royal scandals.
top_five_star_obscene.title: 5 Bastard Loving stars

top_five_star_obscene.text:
The desire to reunite with his ex-wife, Mary, and their daughter, Maggie, is the driving force that has Dick and his companions leaving Chicago. Dick has promised to get Jamie and her daughter, Mel, home to their family in Nebraska. Courtney and Luis have invited themselves along in hopes of making it to Texas. With the area of Chicago they are leaving somewhat under control, the group faces new and unspeakable horrors along their journey.

Craven’s second book in the Out Of The Dark series, The Devil’s Road, is a fast-paced, hard-hitting adventure rich with challenges and new characters. Dick’s character is brought face-to-face with the worst parts of his past.

This book was emotionally draining for me. I truly felt for Dick and the tortuous fate he endured. I hope book three brings him peace.

5 out of 5 stars.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Aptly Named

top_five_star_obscene.text:
Merry Blissmas (Biker Bitches #3)
By: Jamie Begley
5/5 stars

Bliss has been the long hated female character in this series. I really did hate her several times during this series and I really thought she needed a beat down a time or two. But when she was kicked out of the Last Rider’s during Lucky’s Choice, I genuinely felt sorry for her. The last two outbursts she had were because she felt that the men had been wronged by their new, outsider women. I knew that she had to have a messed up life history and I was right. This book changed the way I viewed Bliss and my feelings about her.

Drake, a single father & first cousin to the Porter clan, has always had his eye on Bliss, even though she never noticed him or gave him the time of day. Part of that is because as a female member of the Last Riders, she’s not allowed to be with anyone but a Last Rider. But Bliss never had cause to look outside the club either. After Bliss was ousted from the Last Riders, she stayed in town. Drake knows that he has a chance and is willing to date Bliss and finally get her for himself. I love that Drake doesn’t care about her sexual history and only cares about making a future with her.

Their relationship isn’t easy, it’s hard fought for and boundaries are learned and tested. Several questions are answered, some more are posed and more answers given. There were things they did for each other that were super sweet. But Drake is possibly the most perfect alpha, who only has a moment or two of jackassery, but always comes out thoughtful. The things he did for Bliss were just perfection and the type of thing you’d want a man to do for you.

I like the possibilities of love matches that were hinted at between several of the characters. I absolutely loved the epilogue and am intrigued that Jamie Begley may write books for the all the kids from these series. I also like the teaser to Penny & Jackal’s book.

top_five_star_obscene.title: Drake is perfection and Bliss even changed my feelings

top_five_star_obscene.text:
Big Fat Bitch is utterly fantastic! Ginger Voight truly digs deep for this story of a powerful woman in Hollywood who needs to find her inner strength to be just as mighty in her personal life. Sofie Vincent is a force to be reckoned with, show-runner extraordinaire on the set of Vindication, everyone shaking in their boots should they miss a line or come to work not their best. I was even scared for them at first since she rules that set with an iron fist. But then we get the backstory. The reasons behind Sofie's actions and slowly but surely that brick wall around her heart, her soul and her size 14 body finally start to crumble.

At the center of her new outlook on life, albeit a painful one, is the unassuming but very sexy nurse that is Fletcher Sullivan. Voight gives Sofie just the kind of man she never even knew she needed when Fletcher comes waltzing into her life, threatening to expose her alter ego and peeling back the layers of her hardened heart. I just love him. The way he isn't afraid to go toe to toe with the big fat bitch and show her a world that doesn't have to exist on hiding her true self under a coat of armor. I could sense the electricity right from the start. And it is very slow burn kind of romance. And this is ok because this book is not about their love affair. Instead it is Sofie's journey to accept what she cannot change and embrace what is the inevitable. Her less than stellar relationship with her mother is at the core of this story and I found myself tearing up over and over again at the pain that Sofie felt at the hands of her mother's words. And not to give anything away but it's not the spoken word but the written one.

This soul-searching odyssey for Sofie Vincent is one filled with an all-consuming denial based on what she perceives as the world's opinion of her when she was young. And I loved that the Author used Fletcher's daughter as the bridge between the old Sofie and and the new Sofia. One proud to carry her family's name and one proud of her lineage. This is not an easy road and Sofie has many stops and starts as she emerges from the ball-busting tyrant to the woman she has secretly always wanted to be. Along the way, she is surrounded by the most wonderful characters with Fletcher, Ava, Davina, Vincent and Rose adding the perfect touch to this tender story. And it is tender. It is heartbreaking. But it is beautiful and as usual, the Author puts a bit of herself into her heroine and gives us her take on what it is like to be a bit zaftig but still take the world by storm. Ginger Voight does that with each of her books, always providing a heroine who is much more worthy of true happiness than she thinks. Heroines who are gorgeous inside and out if they just take the time to look.

Big Fat Bitch is one of Voight's best to date with what feels like a zillion easter eggs for the taking. I love that she extends her fictional worlds to include the new kid on the block (so to speak) with each new story and how they continue to make me sigh with their random acts of kindness and sure smiles.

Ginger Voight always delivers and Big Fat Bitch is no exception. I'm thrilled she is back and happily give this latest of hers a Big Fat 5 Stars!
top_five_star_obscene.title: Beautifully-written tale of empowerment and finding love when you need it most!

top_five_star_obscene.text:
*** 6 Star Review ***
*** 2017 Favorite ***
Wow! Renee Carlino astounded me and left me emotionally floored in a very good way. It took a lot of ugly tears and emotional hiccups along the way but the end result was so worth the emotional journey. The epilogue validated every single reason I believe in love.

I can honestly say that I suffered a very serious book hangover for days after reading this book.

The first chapter introduces the reader to Charlotte, a twenty seven year old woman still struggling to find her niche in life. She’s had as many bad jobs as she’s had bad relationships. She’s educated and has tried many different types of jobs. Nothing seems to stick. She’s still working a crappy waitress job with crappy uniforms, crappy tips, and a crappier boss. She’s become perpetually stuck in life. Even though her life is less than stellar, she is eternally optimistic and is still searching for the right fit. She’s constantly comparing her non achievements in life to her over achieving, younger brother who’s on the road to success. Her parents are bewildered by her life choices. Her best friend, who is also perpetually stuck in her twenties, is her biggest cheerleader and lifetime roommate in their crappy apartment. One bad breakup ( the BFF) leads to a night that changes the course of her life. She meets a random stranger on the street and immediately makes a connection that leads to the most unusual but very memorable one night stand.

An adventurous one night stand that is filled with spontaneity, maple donuts, and forgotten Chinese food leads to a confusing and somewhat degrading walk of shame the next morning. Charlotte can’t seem to forget the night she spent with Adam, the vivacious artist with a thirst for life. His spontaneity and pure joy marked her soul.

Months later, she’s finally ready to move on when she meets a sexy baseball player, Seth, on an online dating site. What begins as a fun flirtation starts developing into a real relationship that has potential of being the very thing she’s been searching for. Everything is perfect until it’s not. There’s something holding her back and that something is her one night stand with Adam. From that point on everything begins to change and spiral out of control. Her best friend is moving on in life and grasping the adulting concept. Her successful baby brother has become her new life coach and roommate. Seth has made it to the major leagues and wants her along for the ride. The problem is that Charlotte is still stuck in her life. Maybe she needs to go back to the beginning or back to the one night that still haunts her thoughts. She needs some kind of closure from the disastrous one night stand that muddled her direction in life. The answers aren’t what she was expecting nor are they black and white. Adam’s life is anything but carefree and spontaneous. He’s a different man than the one she met on the street. Now that she has the answers, the choices are hers to make. Does she let go of Seth, the man who offers her everything she’s looking for in life or does she take the risk and follow Adam on a spontaneous adventure that has no guarantees of a future?

Choices are never easy and many times they aren’t right or wrong. They are merely stepping stones in the journey of life. The most important thing is accepting that choice and letting it become part of life’s compass. Eventually it will lead Charlotte to the place she was meant to be.

I’m usually not a fan of love triangle themed romances but this novel was an unusual triangle that had all the “feels” from every angle. I loved both men equally and swooned over them both. What I loved most is how they both loved Charlotte. They both loved her unconditionally, without measure, or expectations. They accepted her as she was. They both encouraged her to become something better for herself rather than for them. They wanted her to have everything she deserved in life. Most of all, they wanted her to know she was loved no matter what happened in her life. They both gave her pure unconditional love.

I’m choking up with tears as I write this review. Thinking about all that Charlotte experienced, the pain, joy, and rebirth, was phenomenal. This novel gave Charlotte the potential to experience all of the possibilites offered in life. I think we all strive to experience the pure love Charlotte received.

Wish You Were Here by Renee Carlino is more than a romance novel, it is an amazing reading adventure. It has become one of my all time favorite books.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Wish You Were Here by Renee Carlino is more than a romance novel, it is an amazing reading adventure

top_five_star_obscene.text:
Absolutely loved this book/chapter of the Royal Bastards MC. Ms. Knight has given us a very well written book with characters I simply loved. The female lead (Alice) is a strong, independent woman who comes into her own and realizes the life she has led is not the life she wants in the future but can she reach out and take what she wants or will she succumb to her father’s wishes? As for the male lead (Reaper), he is the prez of the Texas Chapter of the Royal Bastards and trust me he fits the “B*stard mold to a tee. He’s not a good man. He does things that need to be done and not necessarily things most people would be proud of. Yet he does them with a blink of an eye and no regrets. When it comes to his club and his brothers he is loyal to a fault and does whatever it takes to keep them solid and living a happy MC lifestyle.

Alice and Reaper meet while she is looking for her sister, who has run away - again. Reaper knows where she is but doesn’t just hand over information, at least not without getting something in return and that something in this case is Alice. What happens from here is one exciting, romantic, and thrilling ride. I highly recommend this chapter of the Royal Bastards Series and this reader is anxiously waiting for book 2 in the Royal Bastards, Texas Chapter.

2 Thumbs Up and 5+ Stars.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Just an Awesome read!!!! WANT MORE from this Texas Chapter

top_five_star_obscene.text:
Thief is amazing and brilliant! I love the entire Love Me with Lies Series: The Opportunist, Dirty Red and now Thief.

In Thief we FINALLY get to Caleb's side of the story and what a side that is!! I feel bad for the guy. He has had to deal with two women who both had their own shares of issues. Olivia is broken and trust isn't something she gives away easily - and with good read. Leah is just a bitch! In Dirty Red we did get to see why that is but sorry this girl will always think Leah is pure bitch. But she is a bitch whose story was worth knowing.

Back to Thief. The ENTIRE read was one of constant stress for me. I heard or read somewhere that Tarryn doesn't like HEA (I don't know if this is true) so for the entire book I stressed myself out hoping that I would read the ending I so desperately wanted-Caleb and Olivia finally ending up together with their baby and riding off into the sunset. I am not saying if I got my wish granted but I will say that Tarryn keeps you guessing and hoping for the whole book.

Caleb finally decides to fight for Olivia. I was so happy. In the other two books I was so upset when he would just let her go. So much could've been resolved if he would've just went after her!!! In Thief he really gives it his all.

This doesn't make getting her (if he gets her at all) easy. They have a lot of history together and from their individual lives that get in the way - over and over again. It was enough angst and stress to have me reaching for some Tums or better yet a bottle of wine to help numb it all! Not only did I need a glass or two of wine to get me through this book but I also needed a box of tissues because there is some very heart breaking parts to this book. You won't see it coming.

I had to put this book down a couple of times-not because it was bad -- but because Tarryn know exactly how to pull you into a storyline and then turns her focus onto another important storyline. Made me scream often out of frustration but its a frustration that was so worth it. As much as I bitch when I am reading a story like this I secretly love it! I am sort of sad that these stories are over. What does that say about me, that I want to continue to be tortured right along with Olivia, Caleb and Leah?

Without spoiling anything for you, in my opinion, it is NOT your typical ending to a series.
top_five_star_obscene.title: 5+ nail biting, truck driver cursing, wine guzzling

top_five_star_obscene.text:
Buy the Kindle Set for this series. You gat all 5 books for the price of three!

Book One: Winter's Heat is the story of Rannulf and Rowena. I love their story, so be sure to read it first. 5 stars for Winter's Heat

Book Two: Summer's Storm is the story of Rannulf's older brother Temric and Rowena's older sister, Phillipa, both of whom are bastards. As much as I looked forward to Temric's story, I thought it lacking primarily because I didn't really find Phillipa very likeable. So I would say 3 stars for Summer's Storm.

Book Three: Spring's Fury leaves off where Winter's Heat ends and is the story of the youngest FitzHenry brother, Gilliam and Nicola Ashby. Nicola is John Ashby's daughter that you meet in Book One. She becomes Rannulf's "ward" after Gilliam kills her father after her father imprisons Rannulf and Rowena. This is my FAVORITE of the series. I absolutely LOVED Gilliam and his wicked wit. Nicola is one that you don't really like at first but soon come to love as well. Plus you meet Geoffrey, Gilliam's true full-blooded brother and Jocelyn of Freyne whose mother, Elyssa, becomes the heroine in Autumn's Flame.

Book 4: Autumn's Flame overlaps with Spring's Fury in the beginning and is the story of Geoffrey and Elyssa. I really enjoyed this book. It would be my third favorite of the series behind Spring's Fury and Winter's Heat. At first I didn't like Elyssa. She was too clingy with her son Jocelyn and she cried way too much, but she eventually comes around and defends her castle during a siege like a truly resilient heroine. Geoffrey is just beautiful. Scarred and tortured by his past, unfeeling and cold, he mourns that his daughter is terrified of him and rumors of his being the Devil's spawn abound. He is the sheriff of his shire and by default becomes Elyssa's and Jocelyn's warden after her husband is killed by a boar in a hunting accident. Since Elyssa is newly pregnant, he is also warden to her unborn child and must be present during the birthing process to ensure the rightful child is given the deceased father's lineage and heritage. The fact that he falls in love with Elyssa while she grows great with another man's child is of itself, lovable. There's just something about the tortured, scarred, hero whose physical imperfections house a beautiful soul. The tortured and scarred hero is so very relevant today with so many of our soldiers coming home scarred from IED's in Afghanistan and Iraq.

Book 5: A Love For All Seasons is the story of another FitzHenry, bastard son Rob of Blacklea and Johanna. In this book we travel back to Stanrudde, the merchant town of Temric, Jehan, and Peter of Book 2 Summer's Storm. It was a bit disconcerting as the chapters of this book go back and forth between the years when they are kids and those of adulthood. When Rob's mother dies and his "father" discovers he is the bastard son of Henry of Graistan, his father beats him and sells him to a merchant, Johanna's father. What ensues is that Johanna is already promised to Katel, another of her father's apprentices. In secret, Johanna and Rob exchange marriage vows. Her father and Katel convince her that Rob was offered payment to leave her. Sixteen years later Rob returns as Katel announces to Stanrudde's council that his wife hasn't been in a convent for the past months but has instead been in an adulterous relationship with Rob. Rob knows that Katel has committed crimes worthy of death and if discovered Johanna will suffer the same penalty. Katel just wants Johanna's property that is entrusted for her son Peter. But the only way he can get it is to prove she has been unfaithful. There are plenty of twisty plots in this storyline that keep you hooked in spite of not really liking Johanna and that Rob refuses to admit he another FitzHenry bastard. Temric, aka Richard of Graistan, also plays a major role in setting things right for his newly found half-brother. The only thing I didn't like about the story was that Temric hadn't yet told Rannulf, Geoffrey, or Gilliam of his their new sibling by the books end. Since Rob is described as looking more like Rannulf with gray eyes but excessively tall like Gilliam, the introductory meeting would have been worth reading instead of being left to one's imagination. I would give this book 4 stars. It would be 5 if it weren't for the back and forth between then and now.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Book 4 of The Seasons Series

top_five_star_obscene.text:
I freaking loved this book, like really loved it something hard core!

Let me start by saying this book is based on the author’s real life experiences and her story is set out in a diary/ day to day format which makes for a fantastic unique MC story.

Kudos to Bink for writing about her life in the MC and I can only imagine the heartbreak and memories that she would have brought up to the surface when relaying her story.

Early on you can see that Bink and Big Dick (BD) had a protector/father relationship but as she grew older and you read the flashback scenes, it’s apparent that she means more to him than a father/daughter type relationship. She has always had a calming effect on Big Dick and can calm him down from any irate situation. Her attachment to Big Dick and the rest of the Club Members would have made her decision at the end extremely difficult but for her own sanity, she did what she thought was best for her and that takes a lot of balls to do what she did on her own.

The emotion in the story was so real that I felt like I was actually in Bink’s shoes the entire time. I laughed with her, I felt intense anger with her, I was confused with her and I felt every sexy scene between her and Big Dick.

I am going to be left wondering what happens next and what was supposed to happen at the 50th birthday party until Volume Two releases.

An absolutely flawless debut from Bink!

I was given a complimentary copy in exchange for an honest review. Reviewed by Tanya.
top_five_star_obscene.title: A Flawless Debut!

top_five_star_obscene.text:
What a book!! We loved every page we laughed at the sassy bitchy Aubrey and our hearts broke for the beautiful sexy cocky bastard Chance.
Who doesn’t love us Aussies and our accent (as the US say) we have our own sort of Aussie slang!

Road trip who doesn’t love one of them maybe not with a complete stranger but this is Aubrey and Chance’s story!!

Aubrey is on her way to California from Chicago with her car jammed pack with all her belongings, she takes a breather to re fuel and grab some lunch, that where fate collides.

She meets him.

She need him to fix her tire.

He needs a lift.

There’s a spark.

“What’s C.B. stand for anyway?” “Those are my initials.” “Let me guess…Cocky Bastard.” “Chance Bateman. Some of my friends call me Cocky.”

There road trip is full of so many hysterical moments we have magic wand vibrators, USA president bobble heads, a goat and the wonderful what happen in Vegas stays in Vegas sin city stay over that comes with its very own wedding!
After the road trip is cut short Aubrey is heart broken

She wants him

His gone

No trace

No sign

Gone……

2 years later……

“They say if you let something go and it doesn’t come back to you, it was never really yours to begin with.”

He looks for her.

Has she moved on?

Is he too late?

We loved loved this book it was hilarious, heart breaking and had us rooting for the cocky bastard all the way!!
Thanks for an amazing read!!
top_five_star_obscene.title: We loved every page we laughed at the sassy bitchy Aubrey ...

top_five_star_obscene.text: I am so pissed I didn't know about this author.  I learned about her from authors uncut and Alessandra Torre.  Pissed that my kindle never recommended her, pissed that I  wasted my time on bad books and pissed that this book is over.  Loved this book.  Characters were so well done that I just lost myself in their story.  So so so HAPPY that  I now can devote myself to her other books.
top_five_star_obscene.title: Where to start

top_five_star_obscene.text:
‘Rushing the Goal’ is the 8th book of the ‘Assassins’ series. This book is like a crossover book between ‘Bellevue Bullies’ and ‘Assassins’. The hero and heroine of the book are Benji Paxton and Lucy Sinclair. Ohh and we also have our little heroine Angie :)

If you read the ‘Bellevue Bullies’, you already know who Lucy Sinclair is. She grew up in a loving family but everything changed for her when she got pregnant and married ‘Rick the Dick’. Yeah be ready for murderous thoughts to be awakening by ‘Rick the Dick’. He is the king of a.holes. I hate him with all my heart. After a horrible marriage and suffering through a nasty divorce, Lucy is left angry and bitter. She is also really strong and independent. After Elli Lucy is my favorite heroine from the series. Her only concern is her daughter Angie. She has three younger brothers whom are professional hockey players so when Angie wants to play hockey, Lucy agrees to it and that is how our story really starts :)

At Angie’s first practice Jayden, who is Lucy’s middle brother and the captain of Assassins, promises to be there to help Angie to get ready. But because Jayden has to be at the hospital with his wife, he asks Benji to go and help Angie to get ready. When Lucy realizes that Jayden send Benji on his behalf, she is super pissed and really mean to Benji but somehow she lets him help Angie.

Benji is really sweet, loving, loyal, dependable and a little nerdy. He is perfect and I loved him. He is also an alcoholic, who has been sober for 12 years. The moment he meets with Lucy and Angie, he wants them in his life. But Lucy being so stubborn and not wanting a man in their life, she gives Benji a hard time. I loved and adored Benji and Angie’s relationship. He is really good with Angie and deserves to be her father for that little angel way more than 'Rick the Dick’. I think this is one of the reasons it makes it easier for Lucy to accept him in their life. Both Lucy and Benji is broken and damaged because of their pasts. I loved the way they help each other heal and move past their hurt.

‘Rushing the Goal’ is an emotional and sweet story, which gives you hope for a better future.

Would I recommend this book: Yes.
Would I re-read this book: Already did.
Would I read future books by this author: Yes.
top_five_star_obscene.title: ‘Rushing the Goal’ is an emotional and sweet story, which gives you hope for a better future.
